items.prompt: Should adults be allowed to smoke in public places if everyone nearby knows the risks?

ChatGPT.reasoning: Even if everyone nearby knows the risks, secondhand smoke can harm others involuntarily, so smoking in public places should be restricted to protect public health. Personal knowledge does not eliminate the impact on others.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: Even if everyone nearby knows the risks, secondhand smoke can harm others involuntarily, so smoking in public places should be restricted to protect public health. Personal knowledge does not eliminate the impact on...

Claude.reasoning: Knowledge and consent don't eliminate secondhand smoke's involuntary health harms—children and bystanders can't meaningfully consent. Public health protections appropriately restrict activities causing direct harm to non-consenting people.
Claude.reasoningPreview: Knowledge and consent don't eliminate secondhand smoke's involuntary health harms—children and bystanders can't meaningfully consent. Public health protections appropriately restrict activities causing direct harm to...

Gemini.reasoning: Even if individuals are aware of the risks, the involuntary exposure to secondhand smoke by others in public places infringes upon their right to a smoke-free environment. Public health considerations generally outweigh individual liberty in this context.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: Even if individuals are aware of the risks, the involuntary exposure to secondhand smoke by others in public places infringes upon their right to a smoke-free environment. Public health considerations generally outweigh...

Grok.reasoning: No, secondhand smoke harms non-consenting bystanders' health, violating shared public space rights despite awareness of risks.
Grok.reasoningPreview: No, secondhand smoke harms non-consenting bystanders' health, violating shared public space rights despite awareness of risks.

items.prompt: Should adults be allowed to smoke in public places if everyone nearby knows the risks?

ChatGPT.reasoning: Allowing adults to smoke in public places harms bystanders through secondhand smoke, outweighing the benefit of personal freedom, even if risks are known. Public health and aggregate well-being are prioritized.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: Allowing adults to smoke in public places harms bystanders through secondhand smoke, outweighing the benefit of personal freedom, even if risks are known. Public health and aggregate well-being are prioritized.

Claude.reasoning: No—informed consent doesn't eliminate secondhand smoke's documented harms to non-smokers and children who cannot meaningfully consent. The cost of inaction (public health damage) exceeds the cost of reasonable restrictions.
Claude.reasoningPreview: No—informed consent doesn't eliminate secondhand smoke's documented harms to non-smokers and children who cannot meaningfully consent. The cost of inaction (public health damage) exceeds the cost of reasonable...

Gemini.reasoning: No. Even with awareness, the involuntary exposure to secondhand smoke infringes upon the right of others to breathe clean air, regardless of their consent to be nearby.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: No. Even with awareness, the involuntary exposure to secondhand smoke infringes upon the right of others to breathe clean air, regardless of their consent to be nearby.

Grok.reasoning: Public smoking harms others via second-hand smoke, violating the harm principle regardless of risk awareness. No rule permits it in shared spaces.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Public smoking harms others via second-hand smoke, violating the harm principle regardless of risk awareness. No rule permits it in shared spaces.
